member.name: T:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Configuration.ManifestConfiguration
member.summary: Configures the EF Core mapping for to the Manifests table. Defined in Infrastructure to keep the Domain entity free of any EF Core dependencies.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Configuration.ManifestConfiguration.Configure(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.Builders.EntityTypeBuilder{LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Manifest})
member.summary: Applies column types, constraints, indexes, and relationships for the Manifests table.
member.param: Fluent builder for the Manifest entity type.

member.name: T:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Configuration.OrganizationConfiguration
member.summary: Configures the EF Core mapping for to the Organizations table. Keeping this configuration in the Infrastructure layer (rather than as data annotations on the entity) means the Domain layer has zero dependency on EF Core.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Configuration.OrganizationConfiguration.Configure(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.Builders.EntityTypeBuilder{LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Organization})
member.summary: Applies column types, constraints, indexes, and relationships for the Organizations table.
member.param: Fluent builder for the Organization entity type.

member.name: T:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext
member.summary:
The EF Core database context for the Laerdal Implementer. Acts as the single "window" through which all database reads and writes flow.
Each HTTP request receives its own scoped instance of this context (registered via AddDbContext in Program.cs), so changes are isolated per request and rolled back automatically if the request fails before SaveChangesAsync.

member.name: P:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext.Organizations
member.summary: Provides access to the Organizations table.

member.name: P:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext.Manifests
member.summary: Provides access to the Manifests table.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext.#ctor(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptions{LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext})
member.summary: Initializes the context with the options (connection string, provider, etc.) configured via AddDbContext in the DI container.
member.param: EF Core options, including the SQL Server connection string.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext.OnModelCreating(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ModelBuilder)
member.summary: Applies fluent configuration for each entity type. Separating configuration into dedicated classes (rather than using data annotations on the entities) keeps the Domain layer free of any EF Core dependencies.
member.param: The builder used to construct the EF model.

member.name: T:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository
member.summary: EF Core implementation of . All database access for manifests flows through this class. Like OrganizationRepository, the rest of the application only sees the interface, keeping the persistence technology swappable.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.#ctor(LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext)
member.summary: Initializes the repository with the scoped injected by the DI container.
member.param: The EF Core context for this request.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.AddAsync(LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Manifest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Inserts a new manifest row into the database. Called when creating a draft or when publishing creates a new version record.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.GetByIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads a single manifest by primary key with its owning organization, or returns null if not found.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.GetPublishedByOrganizationAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the single manifest for an organization, or null if none exists. This is the primary read path for the downstream training application: it calls this before starting a learner session to obtain the active content snapshot. The query hits the IX_Manifest_OrgStatus index for efficiency.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.GetByOrganizationIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all manifests for the given organization regardless of status. Useful for admin views showing the full version history.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.GetByOrganizationIdAndStatusAsync(System.Guid,LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Enums.ManifestStatus,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns manifests for the given organization filtered to a specific status. For example, fetching only drafts when the admin opens the "edit manifests" panel.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.ManifestRepository.UpdateAsync(LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Manifest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists changes to an existing manifest (e.g., archiving a previously published version, or stamping PublishedAt when promoting a draft).

member.name: T:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository
member.summary: EF Core implementation of . All database access for organizations flows through this class. The rest of the application only ever sees , so swapping to a different persistence layer (e.g., PostgreSQL, in-memory for tests) requires changing only this class and its registration in Program.cs.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.#ctor(LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.LaerdalDbContext)
member.summary: Initializes the repository with the scoped injected by the DI container.
member.param: The EF Core context for this request.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.AddAsync(LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Organization,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Adds the organization to EF's change tracker and flushes it to the database with a single INSERT statement.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.GetByIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads a single organization by primary key, eagerly including its parent and direct children so the caller has the full local hierarchy context. Returns null if not found.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.GetByCodeAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Finds an organization matching both and (which may be null for root orgs). EF Core correctly translates a null parentId to WHERE ParentId IS NULL. Used by the command handler to enforce code uniqueness before inserting.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.GetAllAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all organizations, each with their parent and direct children loaded. Used by the list endpoint and the ?parentId-less variant of the query.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.GetByParentIdAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all direct children of the specified parent without loading their own children — suitable for shallow list views.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.UpdateAsync(LaerdalImplementation.Domain.Entities.Organization,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Marks the entity as modified in EF's change tracker and persists the changes with an UPDATE statement. The entity must already be tracked or re-attached before calling this.

member.name: M:LaerdalImplementation.Infrastructure.Data.Repositories.OrganizationRepository.ExistsAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns true if any organization row has the given ID. More efficient than when the caller only needs to verify existence (e.g., parent validation in the command handler).
